paper_title: A Ternary Mixing Model Approach Using Benthic Foraminifer δ13C‐δ18O Data to Reconstruct Late Pliocene Deep Atlantic Water Mass Mixing

methods.description: This method involves visualizing and processing large quantities of paired δ13C-δ18O data, averaged over several Pliocene and Pleistocene time slices, to ensure data quality and consistency with established age models.

steps.description: Visualize large quantities of data in comprehensive plots of paired δ13C-δ18O data, averaged over several Pliocene and Pleistocene time slices.
steps.searchable_summary: create plots of averaged δ13C-δ18O data over time slices

steps.description: Combine new data with previously published high-resolution deep ocean stable isotope records from different ocean basins.
steps.searchable_summary: merge new and existing isotope records

steps.description: Revise age models by visually aligning the δ18O data to the LR04 stack.
steps.searchable_summary: align δ18O data to LR04 stack

steps.description: Define seven separate time slices centered around the MPWP and average the δ18O and δ13C data for each interval.
steps.searchable_summary: average δ18O and δ13C data for defined time slices

steps.description: Neglect averages estimated from fewer than five measurements or with very large standard errors in further analysis.
Correct all records for species-specific offsets following Shackleton et al. (1995).
steps.searchable_summary: apply species-specific corrections to isotope records

methods.method_name: Using Paired δ13C-δ18O Data to Identify Deep-Water End-Members
methods.description: This method identifies deep-water end-members by analyzing the spread of δ13C-δ18O data and estimating end-member values using a ternary mixing model.

steps.description: Assume δ13C and δ18O behave as conservative tracers within the Atlantic basin and express the mixing of end-members by a linear BMM.
steps.searchable_summary: model end-member mixing using linear BMM

steps.description: Estimate end-member values from the spread of δ13C-δ18O data from various ocean sites.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate end-member values from data spread

steps.description: Use the MinBoundSuite algorithm to calculate a range of possible ternary mixing envelopes for each time slice.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate ternary mixing envelopes using MinBoundSuite
